translations.text: That I may do right in what I have neglected.” By no means! It is just a word that he utters. And behind them is a barrier, until the Day they are resurrected.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: that I have left behind. I am likely to do good." Nay, it is merely a word that he is uttering. There is a barrier behind all of them (who are dead) until the Day when they will be raised up.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "So that I may do good in that which I have left behind!" No! (Kalla) It is but a word that he speaks; and in front of them is Barzakh until the Day when they will be resurrected.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: That I may do right in that which I have left behind! But nay! It is but a word that he speaketh; and behind them is a barrier until the day when they are raised.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: that I may act righteously in what I have left behind.’ ‘By no means! These are mere words that he says.’ And before them is a barrier until the day they will be resurrected.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: that I should do righteousness in that I forsook' No! It is only a word which he will speak. Behind them there shall stand a barrier till the Day that they shall be resurrected.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: That I might do righteousness in that which I left behind." No! It is only a word he is saying; and behind them is a barrier until the Day they are resurrected.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: so that perhaps I shall act righteously for the rest of my life." Although he will say so but his wish will never come true. After death they will be behind a barrier until the day of their resurrection.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Haply I may do good in that which I have left. By no means! it is a (mere) word that he speaks; and before them is a barrier until the day they are raised.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: so that I may do good works in the world I have left behind." Never! It is indeed but a meaningless word that he utters. A barrier shall stand behind such people till the Day they are raised up again.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "In order that I may work righteousness in the things I neglected." - "By no means! It is but a word he says."- Before them is a Partition till the Day they are raised up.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: LaAAallee aAAmalu salihan feema taraktu kalla innaha kalimatun huwa qailuha wamin waraihim barzakhun ila yawmi yubAAathoona

101.arabic: فَإِذَا نُفِخَ فِى ٱلصُّورِ فَلَآ أَنسَابَ بَيْنَهُمْ يَوْمَئِذٍ وَلَا يَتَسَآءَلُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: When the trumpet blast is sounded no ties of lineage will hold among them, nor will they ask after one another.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So when the Trumpet is blown – so there will neither be any relationship among them* that day, nor will they ask about one another. (The disbelievers)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: For when the Trumpet is blown, that day there shall be no kinship any more between them, neither will they question one another.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Then when the Trumpet is blown there will be no lineage among them that Day, nor will they ask of each other.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Then, when the Trumpet is blown, there will be no kinship among them that Day, nor will they ask of one another.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: When the Horn is blown, no relations between them will exist on that Day, and they will not ask after one another.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And then no sooner the Trumpet is blown than there will remain no kinship among them that Day, nor will they ask one another.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Then, when the Trumpet is blown, there will be no kinship among them that Day, nor will they ask of one another.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And when the trumpet is blown there will be no kinship among them that day, nor will they ask of one another.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: When the Trumpet is blown, there will be no ties between them on that day, nor will they ask [about] each other.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And when the Horn is blown, on that Day their ties of kindred shall be no more, nor will they ask each other.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So when the Horn is blown, no relationship will there be among them that Day, nor will they ask about one another.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: There will be no kindred relations nor any opportunity to ask about others or seek their assistance after the trumpet has sounded.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: So when the trumpet is blown, there shall be no ties of relationship between them on that day, nor shall they ask of each other.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: When the trumpet is blown, on that Day there will be no ties of relationship between them; neither will they ask about one another:

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Then when the Trumpet is blown, there will be no more relationships between them that Day, nor will one ask after another!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Faitha nufikha fee alssoori fala ansaba baynahum yawmaithin wala yatasaaloona

102.arabic: فَمَن ثَقُلَتْ مَوَٰزِينُهُۥ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُفْلِحُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Only those whose scales are heavier in the balance will find happiness.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Therefore the ones whose scales prove heavy – they are the successful.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Then he whose scales are heavy -- they are the prosperers,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Then he whose balances shall be heavy --these! they are the blissful ones.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Then, those whose scales (of good deeds) are heavy, - these, they are the successful.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Those whose scales are heavy—those are the successful.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: It will be an Hour when those whose scales are heavy, they alone will attain success;

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Then, those whose Scales (of good deeds) are heavy, these! they are the successful.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Then those whose scales are heavy, they are the successful.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Then those whose deeds weigh heavy in the scales—it is they who are the felicitous.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Those whose scales are heavy shall prosper,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And those whose scales are heavy [with good deeds] - it is they who are the successful.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: If the side of ones good deeds weighs heavier on a scale, he will have everlasting happiness,

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Then as for him whose good deeds are preponderant, these are the successful.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: then those whose scales weigh heavy with good works will be successful.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Then those whose balance (of good deeds) is heavy,- they will attain salvation:

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Faman thaqulat mawazeenuhu faolaika humu almuflihoona

103.arabic: وَمَنْ خَفَّتْ مَوَٰزِينُهُۥ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ خَسِرُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ فِى جَهَنَّمَ خَـٰلِدُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: But those whose scales are lighter will perish and abide in Hell for ever.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And the ones whose scales prove light – it is they who put their lives into ruin, remaining in hell forever.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and he whose scales are light -- they have lost their souls in Gehenna dwelling forever,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And he whose balances shall be light--these are they who have lost themselves: in Hell they are abiders.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And those whose scales (of good deeds) are light, they are those who lose their ownselves, in Hell will they abide.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: But those whose scales are light—those are they who have lost their souls; in Hell they will dwell forever.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: and those whose scales are light, those will be the ones who will have courted loss. They will abide in Hell.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And those whose Scales (of good deeds) are light, they are those who lose themselves, in Hell will they abide.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And those whose scales are light are those who lose their souls, in hell abiding.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: As for those whose deeds weigh light in the scales—they will be the ones who have ruined their souls, and they will remain in hell [forever].

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: but those whose scales are light shall forfeit their souls and live in Gehenna (Hell) for ever.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: But those whose scales are light - those are the ones who have lost their souls, [being] in Hell, abiding eternally.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: but if it weighs less, one will be lost forever in hell.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And as for him whose good deeds are light, these are they who shall have lost their souls, abiding in hell

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: But those whose scales weigh light will have ruined their souls; in Hell will they abide.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: But those whose balance is light, will be those who have lost their souls, in Hell will they abide.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waman khaffat mawazeenuhu faolaika allatheena khasiroo anfusahum fee jahannama khalidoona

104.arabic: تَلْفَحُ وُجُوهَهُمُ ٱلنَّارُ وَهُمْ فِيهَا كَـٰلِحُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Their faces will be scorched by flames, and they will grin and scowl within it.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: The fire shall scorch their faces, and they will remain dejected in it.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: the Fire smiting their faces the while they glower there.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: The Fire will scorch their faces, and therein they shall be grinning.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: The Fire will burn their faces, and therein they will grin, with displaced lips (disfigured).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: The Fire lashes their faces, and therein they grimace.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: The Fire shall scorch their faces, exposing their jaws.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: The Fire will burn their faces, and therein they will grin, with displaced lips (disfigured).

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: The fire burneth their faces, and they are glum therein.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: The Fire will scorch their faces, while they snarl baring their teeth.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: The fire lashes their faces and therein are shriveled lips.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: The Fire will sear their faces, and they therein will have taut smiles.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The fire will scorch their faces and they will be groaning therein in pain.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: The fire shall scorch their faces, and they therein shall be in severe affliction.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: The Fire will scorch their faces and they will abide therein with their faces distorted.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: The Fire will burn their faces, and they will therein grin, with their lips displaced.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Talfahu wujoohahumu alnnaru wahum feeha kalihoona

105.arabic: أَلَمْ تَكُنْ ءَايَـٰتِى تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْكُمْ فَكُنتُم بِهَا تُكَذِّبُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: "Were not My messages read out to you? But you denied them."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “Were not My verses recited to you, so you used to deny them?”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: 'What, were My signs not recited to you, and you cried them lies?'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Have not My revelations been rehearsed unto you, and them ye have been belying?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "Were not My Verses (this Quran) recited to you, and then you used to deny them?"

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “Were not My revelations recited to you, and you kept on rejecting them?”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: "Are you not those to whom My revelations were recited, and you dubbed them as lies?"

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "Were not My Ayat recited to you, and then you used to deny them"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: (It will be said): Were not My revelations recited unto you, and then ye used to deny them?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: ‘Was it not that My signs were recited to you but you would deny them?’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: (We shall say): 'Were My verses not recited to you, and did you not belie them'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [It will be said]. "Were not My verses recited to you and you used to deny them?"

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (They will be told), "Were Our revelations not recited to you and did you not call them lies?"

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Were not My communications recited to you? But you used to reject them.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Were not My messages recited to you and did you not reject them?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "Were not My Signs rehearsed to you, and ye did but treat them as falsehood?"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Alam takun ayatee tutla AAalaykum fakuntum biha tukaththiboona

106.arabic: قَالُوا۟ رَبَّنَا غَلَبَتْ عَلَيْنَا شِقْوَتُنَا وَكُنَّا قَوْمًا ضَآلِّينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: They will say: "O Lord, our misery overwhelmed us, so we remained a people astray.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: They will say, “Our Lord! Our ill-fate overcame us, and we were the astray people.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: They shall say, 'Our Lord, our adversity prevailed over us; we were an erring people.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: They will say: our Lord! our wretchedness overcame, us, and we have been a people erring.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They will say: "Our Lord! Our wretchedness overcame us, and we were (an) erring people.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: They will say, “Our Lord, our wretchedness prevailed over us, and we were a people astray.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: They will say: "Our Lord! Our misfortune prevailed over us. We were indeed an erring people.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They will say: "Our Lord! Our wretchedness overcame us, and we were (an) erring people."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: They will say: Our Lord! Our evil fortune conquered us, and we were erring folk.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They will say, ‘Our Lord! Our wretchedness overcame us, and we were an astray lot.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: 'Lord' they will reply, 'adversity prevailed over us and we were erring.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: They will say, "Our Lord, our wretchedness overcame us, and we were a people astray.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They will reply, "Lord, our hard-heartedness overcame us and we went astray.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: They shall say: O our Lord! our adversity overcame us and we were an erring people:

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They will answer, "Lord, misfortune overcame us and we became an erring people.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They will say: "our Lord! Our misfortune overwhelmed us, and we became a people astray!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qaloo rabbana ghalabat AAalayna shiqwatuna wakunna qawman dalleena

107.arabic: رَبَّنَآ أَخْرِجْنَا مِنْهَا فَإِنْ عُدْنَا فَإِنَّا ظَـٰلِمُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Get us out of this, O Lord. If we transgress we will surely be sinful."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “Our Lord, remove us from hell – then if we do the same, we are the unjust.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Our Lord, bring us forth out of it! Then, if we revert, we shall be evildoers indeed.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: O our Lord! take us forth from it: then if we return, we shall be wrong-doers indeed.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "Our Lord! Bring us out of this; if ever we return (to evil), then indeed we shall be Zalimun: (polytheists, oppressors, unjust, and wrong-doers, etc.)."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Our Lord! Bring us out of this. If we ever returned, we would truly be evil.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Our Lord! Take us out of this. Then if we revert (to evil-doing) we shall indeed be wrongdoers."

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "Our Lord! Bring us out of this. If ever we return (to evil), then indeed we shall be wrongdoers."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Our Lord! Oh, bring us forth from hence! If we return (to evil) then indeed we shall be wrong-doers.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Our Lord! Bring us out of this! Then, if we revert [to our previous conduct], we will indeed be wrongdoers.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Our Lord, bring us out of it. If we return (to sin), then we shall indeed be harmdoers'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Our Lord, remove us from it, and if we were to return [to evil], we would indeed be wrongdoers."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Lord, take us out of this and if we sin again, we shall certainly be unjust".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: O our Lord! Take us out of it; then if we return (to evil) surely we shall be unjust.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Lord, deliver us from Hell. Then, if we revert again, we shall definitely be wrongdoers."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "Our Lord! bring us out of this: if ever we return (to Evil), then shall we be wrong-doers indeed!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Rabbana akhrijna minha fain AAudna fainna thalimoona

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: He will say: "Remain condemned in it, and do not speak to Me.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Allah will say, “Remain rebuked in it, and do not speak to Me.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: 'Slink you into it,' He shall say, 'and do not speak to Me.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: He shall say: slink away there unto, and speak not unto Me.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: He (Allah) will say: "Remain you in it with ignominy! And speak you not to Me!"

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He will say, “Be despised therein, and do not speak to Me.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Allah will say: "Away from Me; stay where you are and do not address Me.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: He (Allah) will say: "Remain you in it with ignominy! And speak you not to Me!"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He saith: Begone therein, and speak not unto Me.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: He will say, ‘Begone in it, and do not speak to Me!

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: He will say: 'Slink there in it and do not speak to Me'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: He will say, "Remain despised therein and do not speak to Me.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: He will say, "Be quiet and say nothing."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: He shall say: Go away into it and speak not to Me;

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: God will say, "Stay there and do not speak to Me.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: He will say: "Be ye driven into it (with ignominy)! And speak ye not to Me!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qala ikhsaoo feeha wala tukallimooni

109.arabic: إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ فَرِيقٌ مِّنْ عِبَادِى يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنَآ ءَامَنَّا فَٱغْفِرْ لَنَا وَٱرْحَمْنَا وَأَنتَ خَيْرُ ٱلرَّٰحِمِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: There was a section among My creatures that said: 'O Lord, we believe. Forgive us and have mercy upon us, for You are the best of the merciful.'

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: "Indeed there was a group among My bondmen who said, 'Our Lord! We have accepted faith, therefore forgive us and have mercy on us, and You are the Best Among The Merciful.' ”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: There is a party of My servants who said, "Our Lord, we believe; therefore forgive us, and have mercy on us, for Thou art the best of the merciful."

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily there was a party of My bondmen who said: our Lord! we have believed, wherefore forgive us and have mercy upon us, and Thou art the Best of the merciful ones!

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Verily! There was a party of My slaves, who used to say: "Our Lord! We believe, so forgive us, and have mercy on us, for You are the Best of all who show mercy!"

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: There was a group of My servants who would say, `Our Lord, we have believed, so forgive us, and have mercy on us; You are the Best of the merciful.'

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: You are those that when a party of My servants said: 'Our Lord, we believe, so forgive us, and have mercy on us, for You are the Best of those that are merciful,'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Verily, there was a party of My servants who used to say: "Our Lord! We believe, so forgive us and have mercy on us, for You are the Best of all who show mercy!"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! there was a party of My slaves who said: Our Lord! We believe, therefor forgive us and have mercy on us for Thou art Best of all who show mercy;

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Indeed there was a part of My servants who would say, ‘‘Our Lord! We have believed. So forgive us, and have mercy on us, and You are the best of the merciful.’’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Among My worshipers there were a party who said: "Lord, we believed. Forgive us and have mercy on us: You are the Best of the merciful."

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, there was a party of My servants who said, 'Our Lord, we have believed, so forgive us and have mercy upon us, and You are the best of the merciful.'

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: There was a group of my servants among you who always prayed: Lord, forgive us and grant us mercy; You are the best of those who show mercy.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Surely there was a party of My servants who said: O our Lord! we believe, so do Thou forgive us and have mercy on us, and Thou art the best of the Merciful ones.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Among My servants, there were those who said, "Lord, We believe, so forgive us and have mercy on us. You are the best one to show mercy."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "A part of My servants there was, who used to pray 'our Lord! we believe; then do Thou forgive us, and have mercy upon us: For Thou art the Best of those who show mercy!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Innahu kana fareequn min AAibadee yaqooloona rabbana amanna faighfir lana wairhamna waanta khayru alrrahimeena

110.arabic: فَٱتَّخَذْتُمُوهُمْ سِخْرِيًّا حَتَّىٰٓ أَنسَوْكُمْ ذِكْرِى وَكُنتُم مِّنْهُمْ تَضْحَكُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: But you ridiculed them. So much so that out of (spite) for them you forgot to remember Me and laughed at them.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “So you took them for a mockery until your mocking at them made you forget My remembrance, and you used to laugh at them!”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: But you took them for a laughing-stock, till they made you forget My remembrance, mocking at them.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Then ye took them mockingly, so that they caused you to forget remembrance of Me, and at them ye were wont to laugh.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: But you took them for a laughingstock, so much so that they made you forget My Remembrance while you used to laugh at them!

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: But you made then a target of ridicule, until they made you forget My remembrance; and you used to laugh at them.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: you made a laughingstock of them and your hostility to them caused you to forget Me, and you simply kept laughing.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: But you took them for a laughing stock, so much so that they made you forget My remembrance while you used to laugh at them!

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: But ye chose them for a laughing-stock until they caused you to forget remembrance of Me, while ye laughed at them.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: But you took them by ridicule until they made you forget My remembrance, and you used to laugh at them.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: But you took them for laughingstock, mocking at them, until they caused you to forget My remembrance.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: But you took them in mockery to the point that they made you forget My remembrance, and you used to laugh at them.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: "You mocked and laughed at them until you forgot all about Me.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: But you took them for a mockery until they made you forget My remembrance and you used to laugh at them.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: But you made a laughing stock of them to the point where it made you forget My remembrance; and you went on laughing at them.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "But ye treated them with ridicule, so much so that (ridicule of) them made you forget My Message while ye were laughing at them!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Faittakhathtumoohum sikhriyyan hatta ansawkum thikree wakuntum minhum tadhakoona

111.arabic: إِنِّى جَزَيْتُهُمُ ٱلْيَوْمَ بِمَا صَبَرُوٓا۟ أَنَّهُمْ هُمُ ٱلْفَآئِزُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: I have rewarded them this day for they were constant, and they have come to attainment."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “Indeed this day I have rewarded them for their endurance, so that it is they who are the successful.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Now today I have recompensed them for their patient endurance; they are the triumphant.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily I have recompensed them today for they bear patiently, verily they! are the achievers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Verily! I have rewarded them this Day for their patience, they are indeed the ones that are successful.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Today, I have rewarded them for their endurance. They are the ones who are the triumphant.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Lo! I have rewarded them this Day for their steadfastness, so that they, and they alone, are triumphant."

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Verily, I have rewarded them this Day for their patience; they are indeed the ones that are successful.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! I have rewarded them this day forasmuch as they were steadfast in that they, even they, are the triumphant.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Indeed I have rewarded them today for their patience. They are indeed the triumphant.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Today I shall recompense them for their patience, for it is they that have won.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, I have rewarded them this Day for their patient endurance - that they are the attainers [of success]."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: On this day I have given them their reward for their exercising patience and it is they who have triumphed."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Surely I have rewarded them this day because they were patient, that they are the achievers.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: I have rewarded them this Day for their steadfastness, and it is they who have triumphed."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "I have rewarded them this Day for their patience and constancy: they are indeed the ones that have achieved Bliss..."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Innee jazaytuhumu alyawma bima sabaroo annahum humu alfaizoona

112.arabic: قَـٰلَ كَمْ لَبِثْتُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ عَدَدَ سِنِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: They will be asked: "How long did you live on the earth in terms of years?"

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: He will say, “How long did you stay on earth, counting by the number of years?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: He shall say, 'How long have you tarried in the earth, by number of years?'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: He will say: how long tarried ye on the earth in number of year?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: He (Allah) will say: "What number of years did you stay on earth?"

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He will say, “How many years did you remain on earth?”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Then Allah will ask them: "For how many years did you stay on earth?"

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: He will say: "What number of years did you stay on earth"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He will say: How long tarried ye in the earth, counting by years?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: He will say, ‘How many years did you remain on earth?’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And He will ask: 'How many years did you live on earth'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [Allah] will say, "How long did you remain on earth in number of years?"

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: God will ask them, "How many years did you live in your graves?"

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: He will say: How many years did you tarry in the earth?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: He will ask, "How many years did you stay on earth?"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: He will say: "What number of years did ye stay on earth?"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qala kam labithtum fee alardi AAadada sineena

113.arabic: قَالُوا۟ لَبِثْنَا يَوْمًا أَوْ بَعْضَ يَوْمٍ فَسْـَٔلِ ٱلْعَآدِّينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: They will say: "A day or less than a day. Ask the enumerators of numbers."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: They will say, “We stayed a day or part of a day, so ask those who keep count.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: They shall say, 'We have tarried a day, or part of a day; ask the numberers!'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: -They will say we tarried a day or part of a day; ask those who keep count.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They will say: "We stayed a day or part of a day. Ask of those who keep account."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: They will say, “We remained a day, or part of a day; but ask those who keep count.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: They will say: "We stayed for a day or part of a day. Ask of those who keep count of this."

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They will say: "We stayed a day or part of a day. Ask of those who keep account."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: They will say: We tarried by a day or part of a day. Ask of those who keep count!

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They will say, ‘We remained for a day, or part of a day; yet ask those who keep the count.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: They will reply: 'A day, or part of a day; ask those who have kept count'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: They will say, "We remained a day or part of a day; ask those who enumerate."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They will reply. "We remained for about a day or part of it, but ask those who have kept count".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: They will say: We tarried a day or part of a day, but ask those who keep account.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They will say, "We stayed a day or part of a day. Ask those who have kept count."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They will say: "We stayed a day or part of a day: but ask those who keep account."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qaloo labithna yawman aw baAAda yawmin faisali alAAaddeena

114.arabic: قَـٰلَ إِن لَّبِثْتُمْ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا ۖ لَّوْ أَنَّكُمْ كُنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: He will say: "You stayed there only a moment, if you knew.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: He will say, “You stayed but only a little, if you knew.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: He shall say, 'You have tarried but a little, did you know.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: He will say: ye tarried a little indeed; would that ye had known that:

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: He (Allah) will say: "You stayed not but a little, if you had only known!

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He will say, “You remained only for a little while, if you only knew.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: He will say: "You stayed only for a while, if you only knew that.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: He will say: "You stayed not but a little, if you had only known!"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He will say: Ye tarried but a little if ye only knew.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: He will say, ‘You only remained a little; if only you had known.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: He will say: 'You have tarried a little, did you know?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: He will say, "You stayed not but a little - if only you had known.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: God will say, "You have indeed remained there for a short time. Would that you knew it during your life time.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: He will say: You did tarry but a little-- had you but known (it):

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: He will say, "You only stayed for a little while, if only you knew.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: He will say: "Ye stayed not but a little,- if ye had only known!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qala in labithtum illa qaleelan law annakum kuntum taAAlamoona

115.arabic: أَفَحَسِبْتُمْ أَنَّمَا خَلَقْنَـٰكُمْ عَبَثًا وَأَنَّكُمْ إِلَيْنَا لَا تُرْجَعُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Do you think We created you for nothing, and that you will not return to Us?"

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So do you think that We have created you needlessly, and that you do not have to return to Us?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: What, did you think that We created you only for sport, and that you would not be returned to Us?'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Deem ye that We have created youl in vain and that unto Us ye are not to be returned?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "Did you think that We had created you in play (without any purpose), and that you would not be brought back to Us?"

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Did you think that We created you in vain, and that to Us you will not be returned?”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Did you imagine that We created you without any purpose, and that you will not be brought back to Us?"

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "Did you think that We had created you in play, and that you would not be brought back to Us"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Deemed ye then that We had created you for naught, and that ye would not be returned unto Us?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Did you suppose that We created you aimlessly, and that you will not be brought back to Us?’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Did you think that We had created you only for play, and that you would never be returned to Us'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Then did you think that We created you uselessly and that to Us you would not be returned?"

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Did you think that We had created you for a playful purpose and that you were not to return to Us?"

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: What! did you then think that We had created you in vain and that you shall not be returned to Us?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: "Do you imagine that We created you without any purpose and that you would not be brought back to Us?"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "Did ye then think that We had created you in jest, and that ye would not be brought back to Us (for account)?"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Afahasibtum annama khalaqnakum AAabathan waannakum ilayna la turjaAAoona

116.arabic: فَتَعَـٰلَى ٱللَّهُ ٱلْمَلِكُ ٱلْحَقُّ ۖ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ رَبُّ ٱلْعَرْشِ ٱلْكَرِيمِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Exalted then be God, the King, the Real. There is no god but He, the Lord of the glorious throne.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So Most Supreme is Allah, the True King; there is no God except Him; Lord of the Throne of Honour.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Then high exalted be God, the King, the True! There is no god but He? the Lord of the noble Throne.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: So exalted be Allah, the True King! there is no god but he, Lord of the Throne honoured.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: So Exalted be Allah, the True King, La ilaha illa Huwa (none has the right to be worshipped but He), the Lord of the Supreme Throne!

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: So Exalted is God, the Ruler, the Real. There is no god except He, the Lord of the Noble Throne.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: So, exalted be Allah, the True King! There is no god but He, the Lord of the Great Throne.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: So Exalted be Allah, the True King: None has the right to be worshipped but He, the Lord of Al-`Arsh Al-Karim!

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Now Allah be Exalted, the True King! There is no Allah save Him, the Lord of the Throne of Grace.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: So exalted is Allah, the True Sovereign, there is no god except Him, the Lord of the Noble Throne.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: High Exalted be Allah, the King, the Truth. There is no god except He, the Lord of the Noble Throne.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So exalted is Allah, the Sovereign, the Truth; there is no deity except Him, Lord of the Noble Throne.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: God is the most exalted King and the Supreme Truth. He is the only God and the Lord of the Gracious Throne.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: So exalted be Allah, the True King; no god is there but He, the Lord of the honorable dominion.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Then, exalted be God, the true King, there is no deity except Him, the Lord of the Glorious Throne.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Therefore exalted be Allah, the King, the Reality: there is no god but He, the Lord of the Throne of Honour!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: FataAAala Allahu almaliku alhaqqu la ilaha illa huwa rabbu alAAarshi alkareemi

117.arabic: وَمَن يَدْعُ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلَـٰهًا ءَاخَرَ لَا بُرْهَـٰنَ لَهُۥ بِهِۦ فَإِنَّمَا حِسَابُهُۥ عِندَ رَبِّهِۦٓ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ لَا يُفْلِحُ ٱلْكَـٰفِرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Whoever worships another god apart from God, for which he holds no proof, will have to account for it before his Lord. Verily the unbelievers will not prosper.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And the one who worships any other God along with Allah – of which he does not have any proof – his account is with his Lord; indeed there is no salvation for the disbelievers.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And whosoever calls upon another god with God, whereof he has no proof, his reckoning is with his Lord; surely the unbelievers shall not prosper.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And whosoever calleth, along with Allah, unto anot her god, of whom he hath no warranty, then his reckoning is only with his Lord; verily thrive will not the infidels.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And whoever invokes (or worships), besides Allah, any other ilah (god), of whom he has no proof, then his reckoning is only with his Lord. Surely! Al-Kafirun (the disbelievers in Allah and in the Oneness of Allah, polytheists, pagans, idolaters, etc.) will not be successful.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Whoever invokes another god besides God—he has no proof thereof—his reckoning rests with his Lord. The disbelievers will not succeed.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: He who invokes any other god along with Allah " one for whom he has no evidence" his reckoning is with his Lord alone. Indeed, these unbelievers shall not prosper.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And whoever invokes besides Allah, any other god, of whom he has no proof; then his reckoning is only with his Lord. Surely, disbelievers will not be successful.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He who crieth unto any other god along with Allah hath no proof thereof. His reckoning is only with his Lord. Lo! disbelievers will not be successful.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Whoever invokes besides Allah another god of which he has no proof, his reckoning will indeed rest with his Lord. Indeed the faithless will not be felicitous.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Whosoever calls upon another god, other than Allah, having no proof his reckoning will be with his Lord. The unbelievers shall never prosper.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And whoever invokes besides Allah another deity for which he has no proof - then his account is only with his Lord. Indeed, the disbelievers will not succeed.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: One who worships things besides God has no proof of the authority of such things. God is certainly keeping the record of his deeds. The unbelievers will not have everlasting happiness.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And whoever invokes with Allah another god-- he has no proof of this-- his reckoning is only with his Lord; surely the unbelievers shall not be successful.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: He, who invokes another deity along with God, a deity of whose divinity he has no proof, will be brought to account by his Lord. Certainly, those who deny the truth shall never prosper.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: If anyone invokes, besides Allah, Any other god, he has no authority therefor; and his reckoning will be only with his Lord! and verily the Unbelievers will fail to win through!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waman yadAAu maAAa Allahi ilahan akhara la burhana lahu bihi fainnama hisabuhu AAinda rabbihi innahu la yuflihu alkafiroona

118.arabic: وَقُل رَّبِّ ٱغْفِرْ وَٱرْحَمْ وَأَنتَ خَيْرُ ٱلرَّٰحِمِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Say: "My Lord, forgive and have mercy. You are the best of the merciful."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) say, “My Lord! Forgive and have mercy, and You are the Best of all the Merciful.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And say: 'My Lord, forgive and have mercy, for Thou art the best of the merciful.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And say thou: my Lord! forgive and have mercy, and Thou art the Best of the merciful ones.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And say (O Muhammad SAW): "My Lord! Forgive and have mercy, for You are the Best of those who show mercy!"

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And say, “My Lord, forgive and have mercy, for You are the Best of the merciful.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And say, (O Muhammad): "My Lord, forgive us and have mercy on us, for You are the Best of those that are merciful."

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And say: "My Lord! Forgive and have mercy, for You are the Best of those who show mercy!"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And (O Muhammad) say: My Lord! Forgive and have mercy, for Thou art Best of all who show mercy.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Say, ‘My Lord, forgive and have mercy, and You are the best of the merciful.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And say: 'My Lord, forgive and have mercy, for You are the Best of the merciful'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And, [O Muhammad], say, "My Lord, forgive and have mercy, and You are the best of the merciful."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Muhammad), say, "Lord, forgive me and grant me mercy; You are the best of the Merciful Ones.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And say: O my Lord! forgive and have mercy, and Thou art the best of the Merciful ones.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Say, "Lord, forgive us and have mercy. You are the best of those who show mercy."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: So say: "O my Lord! grant Thou forgiveness and mercy for Thou art the Best of those who show mercy!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waqul rabbi ighfir wairham waanta khayru alrrahimeena
